verses.greek: ἐάν τι αἰτήσητέ ⸀με ἐν τῷ ὀνόματί μου ⸀ἐγὼ ποιήσω.
verses.mistral: Ask in my name, and I will do
verses.kjv: If ye shall ask any thing in my name, I will do it.

verses.greek: Ἐὰν ἀγαπᾶτέ με, τὰς ἐντολὰς τὰς ἐμὰς ⸀τηρήσετε·
verses.mistral: Keep my commands
verses.kjv: ¶ If ye love me, keep my commandments.

verses.greek: κἀγὼ ἐρωτήσω τὸν πατέρα καὶ ἄλλον παράκλητον δώσει ὑμῖν ἵνα ⸀ᾖ μεθ’ ὑμῶν εἰς τὸν ⸀αἰῶνα,
verses.mistral: I will ask the Father and He will give you another Advocate to be with you forever.
verses.kjv: And I will pray the Father, and he shall give you another Comforter, that he may abide with you for ever;

words.gloss: an age, a cycle of time

verses.greek: τὸ πνεῦμα τῆς ἀληθείας, ὃ ὁ κόσμος οὐ δύναται λαβεῖν, ὅτι οὐ θεωρεῖ αὐτὸ οὐδὲ ⸀γινώσκει· ⸀ὑμεῖς γινώσκετε αὐτό, ὅτι παρ’ ὑμῖν μένει καὶ ἐν ὑμῖν ⸀ἔσται.
verses.mistral: The Spirit of truth, whom the world cannot receive, because it neither sees him nor knows him. You know him, for he dwells with you and will be in you.
verses.kjv: Even the Spirit of truth; whom the world cannot receive, because it seeth him not, neither knoweth him: but ye know him; for he dwelleth with you, and shall be in you.

verses.mistral: I will not leave you as orphans; I am coming to you.
verses.kjv: I will not leave you comfortless: I will come to you.

verses.greek: ἔτι μικρὸν καὶ ὁ κόσμος με οὐκέτι θεωρεῖ, ὑμεῖς δὲ θεωρεῖτέ με, ὅτι ἐγὼ ζῶ καὶ ὑμεῖς ⸀ζήσετε.
verses.mistral: Yet a little while and the world no longer sees me, but you see me; because I live, you also will live.
verses.kjv: Yet a little while, and the world seeth me no more; but ye see me: because I live, ye shall live also.

verses.mistral: In that day, you will know that I am in my Father, and you in me, and I in you.
verses.kjv: At that day ye shall know that I am in my Father, and ye in me, and I in you.

verses.greek: ὁ ἔχων τὰς ἐντολάς μου καὶ τηρῶν αὐτὰς ἐκεῖνός ἐστιν ὁ ἀγαπῶν με· ὁ δὲ ἀγαπῶν με ἀγαπηθήσεται ὑπὸ τοῦ πατρός μου, κἀγὼ ἀγαπήσω αὐτὸν καὶ ἐμφανίσω αὐτῷ ἐμαυτόν.
verses.mistral: The one who keeps my commands loves me. The one who loves me will be loved by my Father, and I too will love him and reveal myself to him.
verses.kjv: He that hath my commandments, and keepeth them, he it is that loveth me: and he that loveth me shall be loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will manifest myself to him.

verses.mistral: Reveals itself to us, and not to the world.
verses.kjv: Judas saith unto him, not Iscariot, Lord, how is it that thou wilt manifest thyself unto us, and not unto the world?

verses.mistral: Jesus answered him, "He who loves me will keep my words, and my Father will love him, and we will come to him and make our home with him."
verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto him, If a man love me, he will keep my words: and my Father will love him, and we will come unto him, and make our abode with him.

verses.greek: ὁ μὴ ἀγαπῶν με τοὺς λόγους μου οὐ τηρεῖ· καὶ ὁ λόγος ὃν ἀκούετε οὐκ ἔστιν ἐμὸς ἀλλὰ τοῦ πέμψαντός με πατρός.
verses.mistral: Does not keep my words; and the word which you hear is not mine but of him who sent me.
verses.kjv: He that loveth me not keepeth not my sayings: and the word which ye hear is not mine, but the Father's which sent me.

verses.greek: Ταῦτα λελάληκα ὑμῖν παρ’ ὑμῖν μένων·
verses.mistral: These words I spoke to you while still with you.
verses.kjv: These things have I spoken unto you, being yet present with you.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ παράκλητος, τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον ὃ πέμψει ὁ πατὴρ ἐν τῷ ὀνόματί μου, ἐκεῖνος ὑμᾶς διδάξει πάντα καὶ ὑπομνήσει ὑμᾶς πάντα ἃ εἶπον ⸀ὑμῖν.
verses.mistral: The Paraclete, the Holy Spirit whom the Father will send in my name, he will teach you all things and remind you of everything I have said to you.
verses.kjv: But the Comforter, which is the Holy Ghost, whom the Father will send in my name, he shall teach you all things, and bring all things to your remembrance, whatsoever I have said unto you.

verses.greek: εἰρήνην ἀφίημι ὑμῖν, εἰρήνην τὴν ἐμὴν δίδωμι ὑμῖν· οὐ καθὼς ὁ κόσμος δίδωσιν ἐγὼ δίδωμι ὑμῖν. μὴ ταρασσέσθω ὑμῶν ἡ καρδία μηδὲ δειλιάτω.
verses.mistral: Peace I leave to you, my peace I give unto you; not as the world gives do I give unto you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.
verses.kjv: Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.

words.gloss: I send away, release, remit, forgive, permit

verses.greek: ἠκούσατε ὅτι ἐγὼ εἶπον ὑμῖν· Ὑπάγω καὶ ἔρχομαι πρὸς ὑμᾶς. εἰ ἠγαπᾶτέ με ἐχάρητε ἄν, ⸀ὅτι πορεύομαι πρὸς τὸν πατέρα, ὅτι ὁ ⸀πατὴρ μείζων μού ἐστιν.
verses.mistral: I have told you that I go away and come to you. If you loved me, you would rejoice, because I am going to the Father; for the Father is greater than I.
verses.kjv: Ye have heard how I said unto you, I go away, and come again unto you. If ye loved me, ye would rejoice, because I said, I go unto the Father: for my Father is greater than I.

words.gloss: an untranslatable word that makes a statement contingent

verses.greek: καὶ νῦν εἴρηκα ὑμῖν πρὶν γενέσθαι, ἵνα ὅταν γένηται πιστεύσητε.
verses.mistral: And now I have told you before it comes, that when it happens, you may believe.
verses.kjv: And now I have told you before it come to pass, that, when it is come to pass, ye might believe.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: οὐκέτι πολλὰ λαλήσω μεθ’ ὑμῶν, ἔρχεται γὰρ ὁ τοῦ κόσμου ἄρχων· καὶ ἐν ἐμοὶ οὐκ ἔχει οὐδέν,
verses.mistral: Will not speak much more with you. For the ruler of the world is coming, and in me he has nothing.
verses.kjv: Hereafter I will not talk much with you: for the prince of this world cometh, and hath nothing in me.

verses.greek: ἀλλ’ ἵνα γνῷ ὁ κόσμος ὅτι ἀγαπῶ τὸν πατέρα, καὶ καθὼς ⸀ἐνετείλατο μοι ὁ πατὴρ οὕτως ποιῶ. Ἐγείρεσθε, ἄγωμεν ἐντεῦθεν.
verses.mistral: That the world may know that I love the Father, and as Thou hast commanded me, so do I. Arise, let us go hence.
verses.kjv: But that the world may know that I love the Father; and as the Father gave me commandment, even so I do. Arise, let us go hence.

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize
hence, from this place, on this side and on that

verses.greek: Ἐγώ εἰμι ἡ ἄμπελος ἡ ἀληθινή, καὶ ὁ πατήρ μου ὁ γεωργός ἐστιν·
verses.mistral: I am the true vine, and my Father is the vinedresser.
verses.kjv: I am the true vine, and my Father is the husbandman.

words.gloss: a worker of the soil, husbandman, vine-dresser

verses.greek: πᾶν κλῆμα ἐν ἐμοὶ μὴ φέρον καρπὸν αἴρει αὐτό, καὶ πᾶν τὸ καρπὸν φέρον καθαίρει αὐτὸ ἵνα ⸂καρπὸν πλείονα⸃ φέρῃ.
verses.mistral: Every branch in me that does not bear fruit, he takes away; and every branch that bears fruit, he prunes, that it may bear more fruit.
verses.kjv: Every branch in me that beareth not fruit he taketh away: and every branch that beareth fruit, he purgeth it, that it may bring forth more fruit.

verses.greek: ἤδη ὑμεῖς καθαροί ἐστε διὰ τὸν λόγον ὃν λελάληκα ὑμῖν·
verses.mistral: Already you are clean because of the word which I have spoken to you.
verses.kjv: Now ye are clean through the word which I have spoken unto you.

words.gloss: a word, speech, divine utterance, analogy

verses.greek: μείνατε ἐν ἐμοί, κἀγὼ ἐν ὑμῖν. καθὼς τὸ κλῆμα οὐ δύναται καρπὸν φέρειν ἀφ’ ἑαυτοῦ ἐὰν μὴ ⸀μένῃ ἐν τῇ ἀμπέλῳ, οὕτως οὐδὲ ὑμεῖς ἐὰν μὴ ἐν ἐμοὶ ⸀μένητε.
verses.mistral: Remain in me, and I in you. As a branch cannot bear fruit by itself unless it abides in the vine, so neither can you unless you remain in me.
verses.kjv: Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; no more can ye, except ye abide in me.

verses.greek: ἐγώ εἰμι ἡ ἄμπελος, ὑμεῖς τὰ κλήματα. ὁ μένων ἐν ἐμοὶ κἀγὼ ἐν αὐτῷ οὗτος φέρει καρπὸν πολύν, ὅτι χωρὶς ἐμοῦ οὐ δύνασθε ποιεῖν οὐδέν.
verses.mistral: You are the vine, I am the branches. He who abides in Me, and I in him, bears much fruit; for without Me, you can do nothing.
verses.kjv: I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do nothing.

words.gloss: he, she, it, they, them, same

verses.greek: ἐὰν μή τις ⸀μένῃ ἐν ἐμοί, ἐβλήθη ἔξω ὡς τὸ κλῆμα καὶ ἐξηράνθη, καὶ συνάγουσιν αὐτὰ καὶ εἰς τὸ πῦρ βάλλουσιν καὶ καίεται.
verses.mistral: If anyone does not abide in Me, he is thrown out as a branch and withers, and they gather them and cast them into the fire, and they are burned.
verses.kjv: If a man abide not in me, he is cast forth as a branch, and is withered; and men gather them, and cast them into the fire, and they are burned.

verses.greek: ἐὰν μείνητε ἐν ἐμοὶ καὶ τὰ ῥήματά μου ἐν ὑμῖν μείνῃ, ὃ ἐὰν θέλητε ⸀αἰτήσασθε καὶ γενήσεται ὑμῖν·
verses.mistral: If you remain in Me and My words remain in you, whatever you ask will be granted to you.
verses.kjv: If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: ἐν τούτῳ ἐδοξάσθη ὁ πατήρ μου ἵνα καρπὸν πολὺν φέρητε καὶ ⸀γένησθε ἐμοὶ μαθηταί.
verses.mistral: In this, my Father is glorified that you bear much fruit and become My disciples.
verses.kjv: Herein is my Father glorified, that ye bear much fruit; so shall ye be my disciples.

words.gloss: I glorify, honor, bestow glory on
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: καθὼς ἠγάπησέν με ὁ πατήρ, κἀγὼ ⸂ὑμᾶς ἠγάπησα⸃, μείνατε ἐν τῇ ἀγάπῃ τῇ ἐμῇ.
verses.mistral: Abide in my love.
verses.kjv: As the Father hath loved me, so have I loved you: continue ye in my love.

verses.greek: ἐὰν τὰς ἐντολάς μου τηρήσητε, μενεῖτε ἐν τῇ ἀγάπῃ μου, καθὼς ἐγὼ ⸂τὰς ἐντολὰς τοῦ πατρός μου⸃ τετήρηκα καὶ μένω αὐτοῦ ἐν τῇ ἀγάπῃ.
verses.mistral: If you keep my commandments, you will abide in my love, just as I have kept my Father's commandments and dwell in his love.
verses.kjv: If ye keep my commandments, ye shall abide in my love; even as I have kept my Father's commandments, and abide in his love.

words.gloss: he, she, it, they, them, same

verses.greek: ταῦτα λελάληκα ὑμῖν ἵνα ἡ χαρὰ ἡ ἐμὴ ἐν ὑμῖν ⸀ᾖ καὶ ἡ χαρὰ ὑμῶν πληρωθῇ.
verses.mistral: These words I speak to you, so that My joy may be in you, and your joy may be complete.
verses.kjv: These things have I spoken unto you, that my joy might remain in you, and that your joy might be full.

verses.greek: Αὕτη ἐστὶν ἡ ἐντολὴ ἡ ἐμὴ ἵνα ἀγαπᾶτε ἀλλήλους καθὼς ἠγάπησα ὑμᾶς·
verses.mistral: This is My commandment, that you love one another as I have loved you.
verses.kjv: This is my commandment, That ye love one another, as I have loved you.

verses.greek: μείζονα ταύτης ἀγάπην οὐδεὶς ἔχει, ἵνα τις τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ θῇ ὑπὲρ τῶν φίλων αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Greater love has no one than this, that someone lay down his life for his friends.
verses.kjv: Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends.

words.gloss: he, she, it, they, them, same
he, she, it, they, them, same

verses.greek: ὑμεῖς φίλοι μού ἐστε ἐὰν ποιῆτε ⸀ἃ ἐγὼ ἐντέλλομαι ὑμῖν.
verses.mistral: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
verses.kjv: Ye are my friends, if ye do whatsoever I command you.

verses.greek: οὐκέτι ⸂λέγω ὑμᾶς⸃ δούλους, ὅτι ὁ δοῦλος οὐκ οἶδεν τί ποιεῖ αὐτοῦ ὁ κύριος· ὑμᾶς δὲ εἴρηκα φίλους, ὅτι πάντα ἃ ἤκουσα παρὰ τοῦ πατρός μου ἐγνώρισα ὑμῖν.
verses.mistral: You no longer do I call you servants, for a servant does not know what his master is doing; but I have called you friends, for all that I heard from my Father I have made known to you.
verses.kjv: Henceforth I call you not servants; for the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth: but I have called you friends; for all things that I have heard of my Father I have made known unto you.

but, on the other hand, and
all, the whole, every kind of

verses.greek: οὐχ ὑμεῖς με ἐξελέξασθε, ἀλλ’ ἐγὼ ἐξελεξάμην ὑμᾶς, καὶ ἔθηκα ὑμᾶς ἵνα ὑμεῖς ὑπάγητε καὶ καρπὸν φέρητε καὶ ὁ καρπὸς ὑμῶν μένῃ, ἵνα ὅ τι ἂν αἰτήσητε τὸν πατέρα ἐν τῷ ὀνόματί μου δῷ ὑμῖν.
verses.mistral: You did not choose me, but I chose you and appointed you to go and bear fruit and your fruit remain, so that whatever you ask the Father in my name he may give you.
verses.kjv: Ye have not chosen me, but I have chosen you, and ordained you, that ye should go and bring forth fruit, and that your fruit should remain: that whatsoever ye shall ask of the Father in my name, he may give it you.

words.gloss: an untranslatable word that makes a statement contingent

verses.greek: ταῦτα ἐντέλλομαι ὑμῖν ἵνα ἀγαπᾶτε ἀλλήλους.
verses.mistral: Love one another.
verses.kjv: These things I command you, that ye love one another.

verses.greek: Εἰ ὁ κόσμος ὑμᾶς μισεῖ, γινώσκετε ὅτι ἐμὲ πρῶτον ⸀ὑμῶν μεμίσηκεν.
verses.mistral: Hates you first me However, a more literal and formal translation would be: "If the world hates you, know that it has hated me before it hated you."
verses.kjv: If the world hate you, ye know that it hated me before it hated you.

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize

verses.greek: εἰ ἐκ τοῦ κόσμου ἦτε, ὁ κόσμος ἂν τὸ ἴδιον ἐφίλει· ὅτι δὲ ἐκ τοῦ κόσμου οὐκ ἐστέ, ἀλλ’ ἐγὼ ἐξελεξάμην ὑμᾶς ἐκ τοῦ κόσμου, διὰ τοῦτο μισεῖ ὑμᾶς ὁ κόσμος.
verses.mistral: The world loves its own; yet because you are not of the world, therefore the world hates you.
verses.kjv: If ye were of the world, the world would love his own: but because ye are not of the world, but I have chosen you out of the world, therefore the world hateth you.

verses.greek: μνημονεύετε τοῦ λόγου οὗ ἐγὼ εἶπον ὑμῖν· Οὐκ ἔστιν δοῦλος μείζων τοῦ κυρίου αὐτοῦ· εἰ ἐμὲ ἐδίωξαν, καὶ ὑμᾶς διώξουσιν· εἰ τὸν λόγον μου ἐτήρησαν, καὶ τὸν ὑμέτερον τηρήσουσιν.
verses.mistral: Remember the word that I said to you: No slave is greater than his master; if they persecuted me, they will also persecute you; if they kept my word, they will keep yours as well.
verses.kjv: Remember the word that I said unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord. If they have persecuted me, they will also persecute you; if they have kept my saying, they will keep your's also.

words.gloss: a word, speech, divine utterance, analogy
he, she, it, they, them, same
a word, speech, divine utterance, analogy

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ ταῦτα πάντα ποιήσουσιν ⸂εἰς ὑμᾶς⸃ διὰ τὸ ὄνομά μου, ὅτι οὐκ οἴδασιν τὸν πέμψαντά με.
verses.mistral: These things they will do to you because of my name, for they know not him who sent me.
verses.kjv: But all these things will they do unto you for my name's sake, because they know not him that sent me.

words.gloss: all, the whole, every kind of

verses.greek: εἰ μὴ ἦλθον καὶ ἐλάλησα αὐτοῖς, ἁμαρτίαν οὐκ ⸀εἴχοσαν· νῦν δὲ πρόφασιν οὐκ ἔχουσιν περὶ τῆς ἁμαρτίας αὐτῶν.
verses.mistral: They would not have sin, but now they have no excuse for their sin.
verses.kjv: If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had sin: but now they have no cloke for their sin.

words.gloss: he, she, it, they, them, same
but, on the other hand, and
he, she, it, they, them, same

verses.greek: ὁ ἐμὲ μισῶν καὶ τὸν πατέρα μου μισεῖ.
verses.mistral: Hates me, and my Father also.
verses.kjv: He that hateth me hateth my Father also.

verses.greek: εἰ τὰ ἔργα μὴ ἐποίησα ἐν αὐτοῖς ἃ οὐδεὶς ἄλλος ⸀ἐποίησεν, ἁμαρτίαν οὐκ ⸀εἴχοσαν· νῦν δὲ καὶ ἑωράκασιν καὶ μεμισήκασιν καὶ ἐμὲ καὶ τὸν πατέρα μου.
verses.mistral: They have seen, and also hated both me and my Father.
verses.kjv: If I had not done among them the works which none other man did, they had not had sin: but now have they both seen and hated both me and my Father.

words.gloss: he, she, it, they, them, same
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἀλλ’ ἵνα πληρωθῇ ὁ λόγος ὁ ⸂ἐν τῷ νόμῳ αὐτῶν γεγραμμένος⸃ ὅτι Ἐμίσησάν με δωρεάν.
verses.mistral: They have rejected me, a gift.
verses.kjv: But this cometh to pass, that the word might be fulfilled that is written in their law, They hated me without a cause.

words.gloss: a word, speech, divine utterance, analogy
he, she, it, they, them, same
as a free gift, without payment, freely

verses.greek: ⸀Ὅταν ἔλθῃ ὁ παράκλητος ὃν ἐγὼ πέμψω ὑμῖν παρὰ τοῦ πατρός, τὸ πνεῦμα τῆς ἀληθείας ὃ παρὰ τοῦ πατρὸς ἐκπορεύεται, ἐκεῖνος μαρτυρήσει περὶ ἐμοῦ·
verses.mistral: When the Advocate comes whom I will send to you from the Father—the Spirit of truth who proceeds from the Father—He will testify about me.
verses.kjv: But when the Comforter is come, whom I will send unto you from the Father, even the Spirit of truth, which proceedeth from the Father, he shall testify of me:

verses.greek: καὶ ὑμεῖς δὲ μαρτυρεῖτε, ὅτι ἀπ’ ἀρχῆς μετ’ ἐμοῦ ἐστε.
verses.mistral: And you testify that from the beginning I have been with you.
verses.kjv: And ye also shall bear witness, because ye have been with me from the beginning.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Ταῦτα λελάληκα ὑμῖν ἵνα μὴ σκανδαλισθῆτε.
verses.mistral: These words I spoke to you, so that you may not be offended.
verses.kjv: These things have I spoken unto you, that ye should not be offended.

verses.greek: ἀποσυναγώγους ποιήσουσιν ὑμᾶς· ἀλλ’ ἔρχεται ὥρα ἵνα πᾶς ὁ ἀποκτείνας ὑμᾶς δόξῃ λατρείαν προσφέρειν τῷ θεῷ.
verses.mistral: Separates you will make from one another; but the time is coming that everyone who kills you will think they are offering a service to God.
verses.kjv: They shall put you out of the synagogues: yea, the time cometh, that whosoever killeth you will think that he doeth God service.

verses.mistral: However, I have spoken these things to you so that when their hour comes, you may remember that I said this to you. These things were not told to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
verses.kjv: But these things have I told you, that when the time shall come, ye may remember that I told you of them. And these things I said not unto you at the beginning, because I was with you.

verses.mistral: I am going to Him who sent Me, and none of you asks Me where I go.

verses.kjv: Nevertheless I tell you the truth; It is expedient for you that I go away: for if I go not away, the Comforter will not come unto you; but if I depart, I will send him unto you.

verses.mistral: Regarding judgment, indeed, the ruler of this world has been judged.

verses.kjv: Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you into all truth: for he shall not speak of himself; but whatsoever he shall hear, that shall he speak: and he will shew you things to come.

verses.mistral: All that the Father has is Mine. Therefore I said that He takes from what is Mine and will declare it to you.

verses.kjv: Then said some of his disciples among themselves, What is this that he saith unto us, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me: and, Because I go to the Father?

verses.kjv: They said therefore, What is this that he saith, A little while? we cannot tell what he saith.

verses.greek: ⸀ἔγνω Ἰησοῦς ὅτι ἤθελον αὐτὸν ἐρωτᾶν, καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Περὶ τούτου ζητεῖτε μετ’ ἀλλήλων ὅτι εἶπον· Μικρὸν καὶ οὐ θεωρεῖτέ με, καὶ πάλιν μικρὸν καὶ ὄψεσθέ με;

verses.kjv: Now Jesus knew that they were desirous to ask him, and said unto them, Do ye enquire among yourselves of that I said, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me?

In this verse, Jesus speaks about the approaching hour of judgment and his impending betrayal, but assures that he is not alone, as God (the Father) is with him.

verses.mistral: That which I have given you, hold fast to it, so that they also may be one in us, as You, Father, are in Me and I am in You. May they also be in Us, so that the world may believe that You sent Me. And the glory which You gave Me, I have given them, so that they may be one, even as We are one; I in them and You in Me, that they may be perfected in unity, so that the world knows that You sent Me, and loved them as You loved Me. But all things which You have given Me, I have kept them, and among the things which You gave Me, none has perished except the son of perdition, so that the Scripture might be fulfilled. [John 17:11-12, 20-23 ] However, for the specific verse you provided , the translation is: And I kept them in Your name that You gave Me, and none of them has been lost except the son of perdition, so that the Scripture might be fulfilled.
verses.kjv: While I was with them in the world, I kept them in thy name: those that thou gavest me I have kept, and none of them is lost, but the son of perdition; that the scripture might be fulfilled.
